26.title: Give me the details of that tree

params.query:
SELECT
  id                                                   AS tree_id,
  order_id,  -- Shopify order ID, newly added column
  COALESCE(tree_type,  'unknown')                      AS tree_type,
  COALESCE(tree_name,  '')                             AS tree_name,
  ROUND(lat::numeric,   6)                             AS lat,
  ROUND("long"::numeric, 6)                            AS long,
  /* dates als ISO 8601 strings, leeg als NULL */
  TO_CHAR(planted_date,   'YYYY-MM-DD')                AS planted_date,
  /* purchase_date komt uit Shopify: map die in Zoho Creator, niet hier */
  CONCAT(
    LEFT(COALESCE(area,''), 4),
    RIGHT(REGEXP_REPLACE(lat::text,    '[^0-9]', '', 'g'), 4),
    RIGHT(REGEXP_REPLACE("long"::text, '[^0-9]', '', 'g'), 4)
  ) AS tree_code
FROM trees1
WHERE is_claimed = false
ORDER BY id ASC
LIMIT {{29__lineItems[]quantity}};

params.code:
def split_vals(key):
    v = input_data.get(key)
    if not v:
        return []
    return [s.strip() for s in str(v).split(",") if s.strip()]

# Read inputs (robust to missing fields)
tree_ids      = split_vals("tree_ids")
tree_codes    = split_vals("tree_codes")
lats          = split_vals("lats")
longs         = split_vals("longs")
planted_dates = split_vals("planted_dates")
tree_types    = split_vals("tree_types")
order_ids     = split_vals("order_ids")   # nieuw veld

# Return everything
return {
    "tree_ids": tree_ids,
    "tree_codes": tree_codes,
    "lat": lats,
    "long": longs,
    "planted_date": planted_dates,
    "tree_type": tree_types,
    "order_ids": order_ids
}

47.title: zap a- Shopify → Tokenized Gift Tree Link (SKU 01)

params.code:
# Inputs
locale = input_data.get("locale") or "en"
default = "en"

# 1) Normalize separators + lowercase
loc = locale.strip().replace("_", "-").lower()

# 2) Take the language part (left of '-')
code = loc.split("-")[0]

# 3) Special mappings
if code in ["no", "nn"]:
    code = "nb"

# 4) Allow only whitelisted codes
allowed = ["en","nl","de","fr","fi","es","it","pt","sv","nb","pl","da"]
if code not in allowed:
    code = default

return {"lang": code, "locale_norm": loc}

params.code:
import uuid

# Generate a UUID, convert to hex, take first 10 chars
token = uuid.uuid4().hex[:10]

return {"token": token}

# Get IDs from input
ids = input_data['tree_ids']

# If only one ID, make it a list
if not isinstance(ids, list):
    ids = [ids]

# Strip whitespace, convert to string
ids = [str(i).strip() for i in ids]

# Join into CSV string
csv_string = ",".join(ids)

return {"tree_ids_csv": csv_string}

params.query:
INSERT INTO public.gift_claims
  (token, status, expires_at, number_of_trees, tree_ids, sku, locale, gifter_email, updated_at)
VALUES
  (
    '{{51__token}}',                       -- token
    'unclaimed',
    '{{54__output}}'::timestamptz, -- ISO uit Formatter, bv 2025-09-17T00:00:00Z
    {{47__lineItems[]quantity}}::int,          -- getal, geen quotes
    '{{52__tree_ids_csv}}',                -- "1203,1204"
    '{{47__lineItems[]sku}}',
    '{{47__customerLocale}}',
    '{{47__email}}',
    NOW()
  )
ON CONFLICT (token) DO UPDATE
SET  expires_at      = EXCLUDED.expires_at,
     number_of_trees = EXCLUDED.number_of_trees,
     tree_ids        = EXCLUDED.tree_ids,
     sku             = EXCLUDED.sku,
     locale          = EXCLUDED.locale,
     gifter_email    = EXCLUDED.gifter_email,
     updated_at      = NOW()
RETURNING token, status, expires_at;

params.code:
from urllib.parse import urlencode

base = "https://forms.zohopublic.eu/greenmakombeh/form/Boomtoewijzing/formperma/YsxD8dDY28U-GAIrr5RSsT8UeyXqtshkvEFGebvJ3m8"
INCLUDE_TREE_IDS = False  # keep False for production

def pick(*keys):
    for k in keys:
        v = input_data.get(k)
        if v is not None:
            s = str(v).strip()
            if s != "":
                return s
    return ""

def norm_locale(val):
    v = (val or "nl-NL").strip().replace("_", "-").lower()
    mapping = {
        "nl": "nl-NL",
        "nl-nl": "nl-NL",
        "nl-be": "nl-BE",
        "en": "en-GB",
        "en-gb": "en-GB",
        "en-us": "en-US",
        "fr-fr": "fr-FR"
    }
    return mapping.get(v, val or "nl-NL")

qty_raw = pick("aantalBomen","line_item_quantity","quantity","number_of_trees")
try:
    qty = int(qty_raw or "1")
except:
    qty = 1

locale = norm_locale(pick("locale","customer_locale","shop_locale","buyer_locale"))
sku = pick("sku","line_item_sku","sku_code") or "01"
vn = pick("vnGever","gifter_first_name","customer_first_name","first_name")
an = pick("anGever","gifter_last_name","customer_last_name","last_name")
em = pick("emailGever","gifter_email","customer_email","email")

params = {
    "token": pick("token"),
    "aantalBomen": qty,
    "sku": sku,
    "locale": locale,  # keep full locale for your tracking if needed
}

# NEW: add Zoho `lang` parameter (just the language part)
params["lang"] = locale.split("-")[0] if locale else "en"

if vn: params["vnGever"] = vn
if an: params["anGever"] = an
if em: params["emailGever"] = em

# optional/harmless prefill
status_val   = pick("status","claim_status","gift_status")
expires_val  = pick("expires_at","expiresAt","expiry","expiry_date")
tree_ids_val = pick("tree_ids","treeIds","tree_ids_csv","reserved_tree_ids")
if status_val:  params["status"] = status_val
if expires_val: params["expires_at"] = expires_val
if INCLUDE_TREE_IDS and tree_ids_val: params["tree_ids"] = tree_ids_val

claim_link = f"{base}?{urlencode(params)}"
return {"claim_link": claim_link}

# Retrieve the subject and body templates, defaulting to empty strings if not provided
subj = (input_data.get("subject_tmpl") or "") 
body = (input_data.get("body_tmpl") or "")

# Function to fill in the placeholders in the template strings
def fill(s):
    return (s
      .replace("{first_name}", input_data.get("first_name", ""))
      .replace("{claim_link}", input_data.get("claim_link", ""))
      .replace("{output}", input_data.get("output", ""))
    )

# Prepare the output with the filled subject and body
output = {
    "subject": fill(subj),
    "body": fill(body)
}

params.code:
import json, re, ast

def as_list(v):
    if v is None:
        return []
    if isinstance(v, list):
        return v
    if isinstance(v, str):
        return [] if v.strip() == "" else [v]
    return [v]

def csvify(v):
    """als het één string is met komma's of puntkomma's, splits naar lijst"""
    lst = as_list(v)
    if len(lst) == 1 and isinstance(lst[0], str) and ("," in lst[0] or ";" in lst[0]):
        return [s.strip() for s in re.split(r"[;,]", lst[0]) if s.strip()]
    return lst

def pad_or_broadcast(lst, n, fill=""):
    """maak lijst op lengte n; bij 1 waarde kopiëren we die voor alle rijen"""
    lst = list(lst or [])
    if not lst:
        return [fill] * n
    if len(lst) == 1:
        return [lst[0]] * n
    if len(lst) < n:
        return lst + [fill] * (n - len(lst))
    return lst[:n]

# ids
tree_ids_csv = (input_data.get("tree_ids_csv") or "").strip()
ids = [s.strip() for s in re.split(r"[;,]", tree_ids_csv) if s.strip()]
ids_len = len(ids)

# totaal aantal bomen
try:
    num_trees = int(str(input_data.get("number_of_trees") or 0).strip() or 0)
except Exception:
    num_trees = 0

# velden uit subformulier
first_names = csvify(input_data.get("first_names"))
last_names  = csvify(input_data.get("last_names"))
emails      = csvify(input_data.get("emails"))

# aantallen naar ints, '4,1' -> [4, 1]
aantallen_raw = csvify(input_data.get("aantallen"))
aantallen = []
for x in aantallen_raw:
    s = str(x).strip()
    if s == "":
        aantallen.append(0)
    else:
        try:
            aantallen.append(int(s))
        except Exception:
            digits = "".join(re.findall(r"-?\d+", s))
            aantallen.append(int(digits) if digits else 0)

deliver_on  = csvify(input_data.get("deliver_on"))

# extra velden
tree_name_in        = csvify(input_data.get("tree_name"))
personal_msg_in     = as_list(input_data.get("personal_message"))   # niet csvify, mag komma's bevatten
certificate_type_in = csvify(input_data.get("certificate_type"))
nickname_sender_in  = csvify(input_data.get("nickname_sender"))

# gelijke lengte afdwingen
n = max(len(emails), len(aantallen), len(first_names), len(last_names), len(deliver_on), 1)
first_names += [""] * (n - len(first_names))
last_names  += [""] * (n - len(last_names))
emails      += [""] * (n - len(emails))
aantallen   += [0]  * (n - len(aantallen))
deliver_on  += [""] * (n - len(deliver_on))

# extra velden op lengte brengen
tree_names        = pad_or_broadcast(tree_name_in,        n, "")
personal_msgs     = pad_or_broadcast(personal_msg_in,     n, "")
certificate_types = pad_or_broadcast(certificate_type_in, n, "")
nickname_senders  = pad_or_broadcast(nickname_sender_in,  n, "")

rows = []
for i in range(n):
    rows.append({
        "first_name":        first_names[i].strip(),
        "last_name":         last_names[i].strip(),
        "email":             emails[i],
        "aantal":            int(aantallen[i]),
        "deliver_on":        deliver_on[i],
        "tree_name":         tree_names[i],
        "personal_message":  personal_msgs[i],
        "certificate_type":  certificate_types[i],
        "nickname_sender":   nickname_senders[i],
    })

sum_subform = sum(int(r.get("aantal") or 0) for r in rows)
ok = (sum_subform == num_trees == ids_len)

return {
    "ok": ok,
    "sum_subform": sum_subform,
    "number_of_trees": num_trees,
    "ids_len": ids_len,
    "rows_json": json.dumps(rows, ensure_ascii=False),
    "tree_ids_csv_norm": ",".join(ids)
}

import json, ast, re
from datetime import datetime
from collections import OrderedDict

# ---------- helpers ----------
def _to_list(v):
    if isinstance(v, list): return v
    if v is None or v == "": return []
    return [v]

def _int_or_zero(v):
    try: return int(str(v).strip())
    except Exception: return 0

def _clean_email(s): return (s or "").strip().lower()

def _first_non_empty(*vals):
    for v in vals:
        if isinstance(v, str) and v.strip():
            return v.strip()
    return ""

def _to_iso_date(s):
    s = (s or "").strip()
    if not s: return ""
    fmts = [
        "%Y-%m-%d","%d-%m-%Y","%Y/%m/%d","%d/%m/%Y",
        "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S","%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S","%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%f",
    ]
    for fmt in fmts:
        try: return datetime.strptime(s[:len(fmt)], fmt).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        except Exception: pass
    try: return datetime.strptime(s[:10], "%Y-%m-%d").strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    except Exception: return ""

def _parse_json_maybe_py(s):
    if isinstance(s, list): return s
    txt = str(s or "").strip()
    if not txt: return []
    try: return json.loads(txt)
    except Exception: return ast.literal_eval(txt)

# ---------- input ----------
ids_csv = (input_data.get("tree_ids_csv") or "").strip()
all_ids = [x for x in re.split(r"[,\s]+", ids_csv) if x]      # strings OK
rows = _parse_json_maybe_py(input_data.get("rows_json"))

# ---------- groepeer per e-mail ----------
groups = OrderedDict()
order = 0

for r in rows:
    email = _clean_email(r.get("recipient_email") or r.get("email"))
    name  = _first_non_empty(
        r.get("recipient_name"),
        f"{(r.get('first_name') or '').strip()} {(r.get('last_name') or '').strip()}".strip()
    )
    nickname   = _first_non_empty(r.get("nickname_sender"), r.get("nickname"), r.get("from_name"))
    cert_type  = _first_non_empty(r.get("certificate_type"), r.get("cert_type"))
    message    = _first_non_empty(r.get("personal_message"), r.get("message"))
    deliver_on = _to_iso_date(_first_non_empty(r.get("deliver_on"), r.get("date")))
    # NEW: tree_name from various possible fields
    tree_name  = _first_non_empty(r.get("tree_name"), r.get("tree_label"), r.get("name_for_tree"))

    requested = _int_or_zero(r.get("aantallen") or r.get("aantal") or r.get("requested") or 0)
    if requested <= 0: requested = 1

    if email not in groups:
        groups[email] = {
            "_order": order,
            "recipient_email": email,
            "recipient_name": name,
            "nickname_sender": nickname,
            "certificate_type": cert_type,
            "personal_message": message,
            "deliver_on": deliver_on,
            "requested_count": 0,
            # NEW holders for tree name(s)
            "tree_name": tree_name,          # first non-empty tree name for this recipient
            "tree_names": []                 # unique list of all names seen (optional)
        }
        order += 1

    g = groups[email]
    g["recipient_name"]  = _first_non_empty(g.get("recipient_name"),  name)
    g["nickname_sender"] = _first_non_empty(g.get("nickname_sender"), nickname)
    g["certificate_type"]= _first_non_empty(g.get("certificate_type"), cert_type)
    g["personal_message"]= _first_non_empty(g.get("personal_message"), message)
    g["deliver_on"]      = _first_non_empty(g.get("deliver_on"),      deliver_on)
    # keep first tree_name; also collect distinct names
    g["tree_name"]       = _first_non_empty(g.get("tree_name"), tree_name)
    if tree_name and tree_name not in g["tree_names"]:
        g["tree_names"].append(tree_name)

    g["requested_count"] += requested

# ---------- verdeel ids ----------
remaining = list(all_ids)
items = []

for email, g in groups.items():
    want = g["requested_count"] if g["requested_count"] > 0 else 0
    take = min(len(remaining), want) if want else 0
    assigned = remaining[:take]
    remaining = remaining[take:]

    items.append({
        "recipient_email":  g["recipient_email"],
        "recipient_name":   g["recipient_name"],
        "nickname_sender":  g["nickname_sender"],
        "certificate_type": g["certificate_type"],
        "personal_message": g["personal_message"],
        "deliver_on":       g["deliver_on"],      # YYYY-MM-DD
        "tree_name":        g.get("tree_name", ""),     # NEW -> single name
        "tree_names":       g.get("tree_names", []),    # NEW -> list (optional)
        "tree_number":      len(assigned),
        "tree_ids":         assigned,
        "tree_ids_csv":     ",".join(assigned),
        "requested_count":  g["requested_count"],
        "assigned_count":   len(assigned),
    })

output = {
    "items": items,
    "items_count": len(items),
    "leftover_tree_ids": remaining,
    "all_tree_ids_csv": ids_csv,
}
return output

UPDATE public.trees1
SET
  user_id       = {{74__user_id}},                       -- of de ID uit stap 8
  is_claimed    = true,
  claimed_token = COALESCE(claimed_token, '{{62__token}}'),
  reserved_token= NULL,
  claimed_at    = NOW()
WHERE id = ANY(STRING_TO_ARRAY('{{67__Loop Item Tree Ids Csv}}', ',')::int[])
  AND COALESCE(is_claimed, false) = false               -- nog niet geclaimd
  AND claimed_token IS NULL                              -- geen claim-token gezet
  AND COALESCE(TRIM(reserved_token), '') IN ('', '{{62__token}}')  -- vrij of door dit token gereserveerd
RETURNING
  id, tree_code, lat, "long", tree_type, planted_date,
  user_id, claimed_token, claimed_at;

val = input_data.get("id")
try:

except Exception:

params.query:
UPDATE public.trees1
SET
  user_id       = {{84__user_id}},                       -- of de ID uit stap 8
  is_claimed    = true,
  claimed_token = COALESCE(claimed_token, '{{62__token}}'),
  reserved_token= NULL,
  claimed_at    = NOW()
WHERE id = ANY(STRING_TO_ARRAY('{{67__Loop Item Tree Ids Csv}}', ',')::int[])
  AND COALESCE(is_claimed, false) = false               -- nog niet geclaimd
  AND claimed_token IS NULL                              -- geen claim-token gezet
  AND COALESCE(TRIM(reserved_token), '') IN ('', '{{62__token}}')  -- vrij of door dit token gereserveerd
RETURNING
  id, tree_code, lat, "long", tree_type, planted_date,
  user_id, claimed_token, claimed_at;

SELECT
  id, tree_code, lat, "long", tree_type, planted_date,
  user_id, claimed_token, reserved_token, claimed_at
FROM public.trees1
WHERE id = ANY(STRING_TO_ARRAY('{{67__Loop Item Tree Ids Csv}}', ',')::int[])  -- 7. Loop Item Tree Ids Csv
  AND claimed_token = '{{62__token}}'                                                -- 2. Token
ORDER BY id

SELECT COALESCE(
  json_agg(
    json_build_object(
      'id', id,
      'tree_code', tree_code,
      'lat', lat,
      'long', "long",
      'tree_type', tree_type,
      'planted_date', planted_date,
      'user_id', user_id
    )
    ORDER BY id
  ),
  '[]'::json
) AS rows
FROM public.trees1
WHERE id = ANY(STRING_TO_ARRAY('{{67__Loop Item Tree Ids Csv}}', ',')::int[])
  AND claimed_token = '{{62__token}}';

params.subject: {{100__alert_subject}}
params.content:
Plan lookup failed — no mapping found for this Chargebee plan_id.

What to do:
1) Open the Zap, Step 2 “derive trees_per_year”.
2) Add a row that maps this plan_id to the correct trees_per_year.
3) Re-send the subscription event.

Payload:
{{100__payload_json}}

params.code:
import json
from datetime import datetime, date
from calendar import monthrange

plan_id     = input_data.get("plan_id","")
tpy         = int(input_data.get("trees_per_year") or 0)
started_at  = input_data.get("started_at") or ""
status      = (input_data.get("status") or "active").lower()

# Fallback mapping in case the formatter step was skipped or gave 0
MAP = {
    "1-boom-per-maand-EUR-Monthly": 12,
    "2-bomen-per-maand": 24,
    "9-bomen-per-jaar": 9,
    "15-bomen-per-jaar": 15,
    "18-bomen-per-jaar": 18,
}
if not tpy:
    tpy = MAP.get(plan_id, 12)

# Build a 12-month distribution that spreads tpy as evenly as possible
base, extra = divmod(tpy, 12)
pattern = [base] * 12
if extra:
    step = 12 / extra
    pos = 0.0
    for _ in range(extra):
        idx = int(round(pos)) % 12
        pattern[idx] += 1
        pos += step

# Anchor day from started_at
start_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(started_at.replace('Z','+00:00'))
anchor_day = start_dt.day  # you can clamp to <=28 if you prefer

# Compute pattern index and next allocation date
today = date.today()
months_diff = (today.year - start_dt.year) * 12 + (today.month - start_dt.month)
pattern_index = months_diff % 12

# Next allocation date on/after today at the anchor_day (clamped to month end)
y, m = today.year, today.month
if today.day > anchor_day:
    m += 1
    if m > 12:
        m = 1
        y += 1
last = monthrange(y, m)[1]
next_allocation_date = date(y, m, min(anchor_day, last)).isoformat()

output = {
    "trees_per_year": tpy,
    "pattern_json": json.dumps(pattern),
    "anchor_day": anchor_day,
    "started_at_date": start_dt.date().isoformat(),
    "status": status,
    "pattern_index": pattern_index,
    "next_allocation_date": next_allocation_date
}
return output

params.subject: Tree allocated → {{119__tree_code}} to {{103__email}}

params.query:
WITH bounds AS (
  SELECT
    (date_trunc('month', now() AT TIME ZONE 'UTC') AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')  AS this_month_utc,
    (date_trunc('month', (now() AT TIME ZONE 'UTC') - interval '1 month') AT TIME ZONE 'UTC') AS prev_month_utc
)
SELECT
  id, tree_code, tree_type, lat, "long",
  claimed_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC' AS claimed_at_utc,
  user_id
FROM trees1 t, bounds b
WHERE is_claimed = true
  AND claimed_at >= b.prev_month_utc
  AND claimed_at <  b.this_month_utc
ORDER BY claimed_at;

zaps.title: Send Forest Hero Photo Email (once per user/ p/h) - Forest Photo 2025Q4 – Eénmalige e-mail

129.title: Send Forest Hero Photo Email (once per user/ p/h) - Forest Photo 2025Q4 – Eénmalige e-mail

params.query:
WITH params AS (
  SELECT 'forest_photo_2025q4_live'::text AS campaign
),
base AS (
  -- kies per boom de nieuwste foto die is_primary = true
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (p.tree_id)
    p.user_id,
    p.tree_id,
    p.file_url,
    p.photographer,
    p.caption,
    p.source,
    p.shot_at_utc
  FROM photos p
  WHERE p.is_primary = true
  ORDER BY p.tree_id, p.shot_at_utc DESC
),
unsent AS (
  SELECT
    b.user_id,
    u.email,
    u.first_name,
    b.tree_id,
    t.tree_type,
    b.file_url,
    b.photographer,
    b.caption,
    b.source,
    b.shot_at_utc,
    'https://map.planteenboom.nu' AS map_url,
    pr.campaign,
    /* sneller en schoner dan JOIN + GROUP BY */
    (SELECT COUNT(1) FROM trees1 t2 WHERE t2.user_id = u.id) AS total_trees
  FROM base b
  JOIN users1 u ON b.user_id = u.id
  /* verzeker dat de boom echt aan deze user hangt */
  JOIN trees1 t ON t.id = b.tree_id AND t.user_id = u.id
  CROSS JOIN params pr
  WHERE COALESCE(TRIM(u.email), '') <> ''
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM email_log el
      WHERE el.user_id = b.user_id
        AND el.tree_id = b.tree_id
        AND el.campaign = pr.campaign
    )
)
SELECT *
FROM unsent
ORDER BY user_id, tree_id
LIMIT 1000;

ON CONFLICT (user_id, tree_id, campaign) DO NOTHING
RETURNING id;

params.subject: {{=gives['330591777']['first_name']}}, we hebben een van jouw bomen gefotografeerd 🌳💚
params.content:
Plant N Boom

Er groeit iets moois in jouw bos 🌿

Jouw boom groeide vandaag weer een stukje verder. Joseph maakte er een foto van. Zie wat jij mogelijk maakt in Sierra Leone: Kijk eens wat er in Sierra Leone dankzij jou is ontstaan:

Bekijk jouw kleine bos

De student achter de lens

📸 Foto gemaakt door Joseph, student in Makombeh. Hij maakt maandelijks nieuwe foto's van de bomen die dankzij echte Forest Heroes zoals jij groeien.

Je hebt inmiddels {{=gives['330591777']['total_trees']}} bomen geplant in Sierra Leone 🌍💚 Dit zijn alleen de bomen die jij zelf hebt geplant via je abonnement — dus zonder cadeaubomen of bomen onder een ander e-mailadres. Dankzij jouw steun ben jij een echte Forest Hero. 🌱

Ja, deel mijn boomverhaal 🌳
Geef Joseph een steuntje 📸

Elke boom is een stap naar een sterker Makombeh. Dankjewel dat jij dat mogelijk maakt. 🌿 Joseph blijft jouw bos volgen en stuurt weer nieuwe foto’s. Misschien ken je iemand die ook een boom wil laten groeien? 🌳

Hartelijke groet,
Eamonn
Plant N Boom

Plan lookup failed — no mapping found for this Chargebee plan_id.

What to do:
1) Open the Zap, Step 2 “derive trees_per_year”.
2) Add a row that maps this plan_id to the correct trees_per_year.

params.code:
import json
from datetime import datetime, date
from calendar import monthrange

plan_id     = input_data.get("plan_id","")
tpy         = int(input_data.get("trees_per_year") or 0)
started_at  = input_data.get("started_at") or ""
status      = (input_data.get("status") or "active").lower()

# Fallback mapping in case the formatter step was skipped or gave 0
MAP = {
    "1-boom-per-maand-EUR-Monthly": 12,
    "20-euro-24-bomen-abonnement-EUR-Monthly": 24,
    "750-9-bomen-abonnement-EUR-Monthly": 9,
    "5-euro-6-bomen-abonnement-EUR-Monthly": 6,
    "15-boom-per-maand-EUR-Monthly": 15,
    "15-boom-per-maand-EUR-Monthly": 18,
}
if not tpy:
    tpy = MAP.get(plan_id, 12)

# Build a 12-month distribution that spreads tpy as evenly as possible
base, extra = divmod(tpy, 12)
pattern = [base] * 12
if extra:
    step = 12 / extra
    pos = 0.0
    for _ in range(extra):
        idx = int(round(pos)) % 12
        pattern[idx] += 1
        pos += step

# Anchor day from started_at
start_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(started_at.replace('Z','+00:00'))
anchor_day = start_dt.day  # you can clamp to <=28 if you prefer

# Compute pattern index and next allocation date
today = date.today()
months_diff = (today.year - start_dt.year) * 12 + (today.month - start_dt.month)
pattern_index = months_diff % 12

# Next allocation date on/after today at the anchor_day (clamped to month end)
y, m = today.year, today.month
if today.day > anchor_day:
    m += 1
    if m > 12:
        m = 1
        y += 1
last = monthrange(y, m)[1]
next_allocation_date = date(y, m, min(anchor_day, last)).isoformat()

output = {
    "trees_per_year": tpy,
    "pattern_json": json.dumps(pattern),
    "anchor_day": anchor_day,
    "started_at_date": start_dt.date().isoformat(),
    "status": status,
    "pattern_index": pattern_index,
    "next_allocation_date": next_allocation_date
}
return output

params.subject: Complete your KETSO Academy onboarding
params.content:
Hello {{=gives["364174535"]["First_Name"]}},

Welcome to KETSO Academy.

Please complete your onboarding before joining the online tree planting session.

Use your personal onboarding link below:

{{=gives["364174536"]["onboarding_url"]}}

You can upload:

* a selfie
* or a favourite object if you do not want to show your face

After uploading, please review your image and click “Submit for approval”.

Once approved, your upload may appear in the KETSO Academy student gallery. After this, join the WhatsApp group:
https://chat.whatsapp.com/CqxAGgHO61xIXc48HyssjM

Thank you for joining KETSO Academy.

KETSO Academy
